openingQuery: What pairs with the Ecru overshirt?

chat.content: What pairs with the Ecru overshirt?

The Ecru Overshirt pairs with the Linen Drawstring Trousers (same washed-oat register) and the Pellier Linen Shirt as a base layer. For texture variety, the Knit Tee in undyed cotton works beautifully underneath — softens the structure of the overshirt without breaking the tonal palette.

toolCalls.description: Product-to-product pgvector similarity from Ecru Overshirt
toolCalls.sql:
SELECT p2.name, p2.price, 1 - (p1.embedding <=> p2.embedding) AS similarity
FROM product_catalog p1
JOIN product_catalog p2 ON p2.product_id != p1.product_id
WHERE p1.name ILIKE '%Ecru Overshirt%'
  AND p2.quantity > 0
ORDER BY p1.embedding <=> p2.embedding
LIMIT 5;
toolCalls.resultSummary: 3 companion pieces returned by product-to-product similarity

chat.content: What's the cheapest piece that goes with the overshirt?
The Knit Tee in undyed cotton at $68. Sits $50 below the Drawstring Trousers and reads as a layering piece rather than an anchor.

toolCalls.sql:
WITH companions AS (
  SELECT unnest(ARRAY['Linen Drawstring Trousers', 'Pellier Linen Shirt', 'Knit Tee Undyed']) AS name
)
SELECT MIN(p.price) AS min_price,
       MAX(p.price) AS max_price,
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY p.price) AS median_price
FROM product_catalog p
JOIN companions c ON p.name = c.name;

chat.content: Is the overshirt in stock at Brooklyn?
Yes — 6 of the Ecru Overshirt on the floor at Brooklyn (BK-01) right now. Portland has 9; Austin has 3.

toolCalls.sql:
SELECT warehouse_id, warehouse_name, quantity, restock_eta
FROM inventory_by_warehouse
WHERE product_id = $1
  AND quantity > 0
ORDER BY warehouse_id;

chat.content: If I don't love it, what's the return window?
30 days from delivery for apparel — unworn, tags attached. We'll email a prepaid label; drop it at any carrier. Refunds post within 5–7 business days after we receive it back.

toolCalls.description: Return window + shipping policy for apparel category
toolCalls.sql:
SELECT category, return_window_days, prepaid_label, refund_sla
FROM return_policies
WHERE category = 'Apparel'
LIMIT 1;
toolCalls.resultSummary: 30-day return, prepaid label, 5-7 day refund window

chat.content: Show me one more linen piece under $100.

The Knit Tee in undyed cotton ($68) — technically cotton, not linen. In pure linen under $100 there's one: the Linen Pocket Square ($78). Small piece, outsized effect; rolls up flat in the side pocket of the Weekender.

toolCalls.sql:
SELECT name, brand, price, 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS similarity
FROM product_catalog
WHERE category ILIKE '%linen%'
  AND price < 100
  AND quantity > 0
ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
LIMIT 10;

telemetry.title: Turn 1 · style_match
telemetry.sql:
SELECT p2.name, p2.price, 1 - (p1.embedding <=> p2.embedding) AS similarity
FROM product_catalog p1
JOIN product_catalog p2 ON p2.product_id != p1.product_id
WHERE p1.name ILIKE '%Ecru Overshirt%'
  AND p2.quantity > 0
ORDER BY p1.embedding <=> p2.embedding
LIMIT 5;

telemetry.title: Turn 2 · price_intelligence
telemetry.sql:
WITH companions AS (
  SELECT unnest(ARRAY['Linen Drawstring Trousers', 'Pellier Linen Shirt', 'Knit Tee Undyed']) AS name
)
SELECT MIN(p.price) AS min_price,
       MAX(p.price) AS max_price,
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY p.price) AS median_price
FROM product_catalog p
JOIN companions c ON p.name = c.name;

telemetry.title: Turn 3 · floor_check
telemetry.sql:
SELECT warehouse_id, warehouse_name, quantity, restock_eta
FROM inventory_by_warehouse
WHERE product_id = $1
  AND quantity > 0
ORDER BY warehouse_id;

telemetry.title: Turn 4 · returns_and_care
telemetry.sql:
SELECT category, return_window_days, prepaid_label, refund_sla
FROM return_policies
WHERE category = 'Apparel'
LIMIT 1;

telemetry.title: Turn 5 · find_pieces
telemetry.sql:
SELECT name, brand, price, 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS similarity
FROM product_catalog
WHERE category ILIKE '%linen%'
  AND price < 100
  AND quantity > 0
ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
LIMIT 10;

sections.title: The full journey
paragraphs: Marco moved through five turns and touched all five specialists. Every routing decision was pattern-matched by the Dispatcher in under 70ms; every specialist ran on its own model at its own temperature.

Curator on Claude Opus 4.6 carried the editorial opening. Value Analyst on Claude Haiku 4.5 answered the price question in 144ms. Stock Keeper on Claude Haiku 4.5 at 0.0 confirmed warehouse inventory — the tool you wrote at lunch. Experience Guide on Claude Opus 4.6 handled the return policy with warmth. Style Advisor on Claude Opus 4.6 closed with one more linen piece.

sections.title: The architecture, made visible
paragraphs: The Performance page tells the rest of the story. The Claude Haiku 4.5 turns (Value Analyst, Stock Keeper) ran in 144ms and 172ms respectively. The Claude Opus 4.6 turns (Curator, Experience Guide, Style Advisor) ran between 920ms and 1420ms. An order of magnitude apart.
That variation isn't a bug. It's the lesson: model selection is an architectural decision.
